mnemonics.title: Cloud Service Models Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Think of it as a pizza: IaaS = dough (infrastructure) provided, you add toppings; PaaS = pre-made crust (platform), you add toppings; SaaS = delivered pizza (complete software), just eat!

mnemonics.title: Virtualization Types
mnemonics.content: Bare-metal = Direct contact with hardware (like running on floor); Hosted = Through host OS (like having a house). Type 1 = direct, Type 2 = through host.

mnemonics.title: Cloud Characteristics (NIST)
mnemonics.content: ON RUM: On-demand, Resource pooling, Rapid elasticity, Measured service.

acronyms.usage: Provides virtualized computing resources - servers, storage, networking. Examples: AWS EC2, Azure VMs.
Provides development platform and tools. Examples: Google App Engine, Heroku, Azure App Service.
Cloud-hosted applications accessed via internet. Examples: Gmail, Salesforce, Microsoft 365.
Emulated computer systems running on physical hardware, enabling multiple OS environments.
Managing infrastructure through code for automated, consistent deployments.
Enables communication between software components, crucial for cloud services integration.
System software managing hardware resources and providing services to applications.
Primary computing component that executes instructions, key resource in capacity planning.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a building: Public = apartment building (shared), Private = single family house (dedicated), Hybrid = connected house with guest quarters, Community = shared office building.
Vertical = adding more floors to a building; Horizontal = building more buildings side by side.
Think of a hypervisor as a building manager - one physical building (hardware) with many separate apartments (VMs), each with its own utilities.
VM = separate houses with their own power plants; Container = apartments in one building sharing utilities (OS kernel).

analogies.analogy: Like utilities (electricity, water) - you pay for what you use, no need to build your own power plant.
Like partitioning a large warehouse into smaller storage units, each independently accessible.

analogies.analization: Like a rubber band - stretches (adds resources) when pulled (demand increases), contracts (releases resources) when released.

analogies.analogy: Like an office building with multiple companies sharing the building but having separate locked offices.
Like a traffic signal distributing cars across multiple lanes to prevent congestion.
